title: Trinetreshwar Temple
tagline: Trinetreshwar Temple — spiritual town of Gujarat

overview.short: Trineteshwar temple (Devanagari: त्रिनेतेश्वर महादेव मंदिर) is a Shiva temple of Pratihara style, located in the Tarnetar village of Surendranagar district, Gujarat.
overview.description: Trineteshwar temple (Devanagari: त्रिनेतेश्वर महादेव मंदिर) is a Shiva temple of Pratihara style, located in the Tarnetar village of Surendranagar district, Gujarat. It was built by Mihira Bhoja in the eighth century. The temple is known for its annual fair, Trinetreshwar Mahadev Mela.

topPlaces.name: Tarnetar
topPlaces.description: Tarnetar is a village in the Surendranagar district of the state of Gujarat in India. It is situated near the city of Thangadh.

topPlaces.name: Thangadh
topPlaces.description: Thangadh (Than) is an industrial city of Surendranagar district in the state of Gujarat, India. The municipality is functioning in Than.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Trinetreshwar Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Trinetreshwar Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Trinetreshwar Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Rajkot Airport (58 km) and the nearest railway station is Rajkot Junction (59 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Trinetreshwar Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Trinetreshwar Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Trinetreshwar Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Trinetreshwar Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Trinetreshwar Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Tarnetar, Thangadh.
